title: Man convicted more than 3 decades after raping 2 women at gunpoint in Framingham store
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Stephen Paul Gale, 73, was convicted in a 1989 rape case involving two women at a Framingham store.
Gale was identified as a suspect through advanced DNA technology and arrested in Los Angeles in 2024.
Gale faces sentencing on March 30 for aggravated rape, kidnapping, and armed robbery charges.

title: New Providence basketball coach goes viral for recruiting joke: ‘Just look at my wife’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bryan Hodgson was introduced as the new head coach of Providence men's basketball.
Hodgson previously led South Florida to a 25-9 record and NCAA tournament bid in his lone season.
Known for his recruiting skills, Hodgson joked about them, referencing his wife during the press conference.

title: He’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon for children in foster care
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Alex Castrichini, 37, from Concord, Mass., will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for children in foster care.
Castrichini was inspired by the efforts of Boston CASA, where he serves on the board, after attending their gala.
He aims to raise awareness and support for foster children facing instability and lack of consistent adult support.

title: ‘Children’s lives are at stake’: Boston teachers protest staffing cuts
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston teachers, parents, and students protested outside City Hall against proposed cuts of over 400 school jobs.
Proposed cuts affect educators and support staff for high-needs students, with layoffs potentially impacting multilingual learners and IEP students.
Boston School Committee to vote on a $1.7 billion budget amid public calls for a $48 million investment to avoid cuts.

title: Where ‘No Kings’ protests are happening around Massachusetts this Saturday
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: 'No Kings' protests will occur in Massachusetts this Saturday at over 160 locations including Boston, Cambridge, and Amherst.
Organizers expect these protests, partly sparked by policy criticisms of President Trump, to be the largest in U.S. history.
Massachusetts ranks second in the number of planned protests, following California, with events in locations from Adams to Wareham.

title: ESPN’s ‘Pardon The Interruption’ hosts believe Jaylen Brown should win NBA MVP
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown is a top candidate for NBA MVP, with strong performances in Jayson Tatum's absence, averaging 28.5 points, 7 rebounds, and 5.1 assists.
ESPN's 'Pardon The Interruption' hosts Wilbon and Kornheiser support Brown as the MVP frontrunner, citing his leadership and consistency.
Boston Celtics are 47-24, second in the Eastern Conference, partly due to Brown's contributions despite the team's roster changes.

title: Marshfield police searching for missing 8-year-old boy
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Marshfield police are searching for Callahan Pierce, an 8-year-old boy missing since Tuesday evening near Moraine Street.
Pierce was last seen carrying a backpack and wearing a black Marshfield sweatshirt and black sweatpants.
Residents are urged to check home cameras and avoid the search area to prevent disruption of police efforts.

title: Will James Hagens play for the Bruins this season? Don Sweeney responds.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens signed an AHL amateur tryout with Providence Bruins, transitioning from college to pro hockey.
GM Don Sweeney states Hagens' NHL debut depends on acclimation to pro game, not immediate expectations.
Hagens will make his pro debut with Providence on Wednesday, as Bruins focus on current playoff push.

title: Man convicted, sentenced to life in prison 40 years after teenager found dead in Plymouth forest
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Michael Hand, 69, sentenced to life for 1986 murder of 15-year-old Tracy Gilpin in Plymouth, Massachusetts.
Tracy Gilpin's body was found 20 days after her disappearance in 1986, death ruled as caused by massive skull fracture.
Hand's 2018 confession led to his arrest; kidnapping and assault charges were dropped due to expired statute of limitations.

title: Pedestrian struck and killed on Mass. Pike
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A pedestrian was struck and killed while crossing I-90 East in Ludlow on Monday afternoon.
State Police closed all travel lanes for 30 minutes following the incident near the 54 mile marker.
The victim was transported to a local hospital and died from their injuries; their identity remains undisclosed.
